provider.title: Choose provider
provider.description: Connect any AI model to your home. Start by choosing your provider.

data_description.base_url: Address of your AI provider. Default: http://localhost:11434/v1 for Ollama

model.title: Model
model.description: Choose the model to use for conversations.
model.description_fetch_failed: Could not connect to {fetch_failed} — enter your model name manually (e.g. llama3.2:3b).

data_description.api_key: Optional for local providers like Ollama and llama.cpp

data_description.web_search_enabled: AI Plugin will search the internet to answer questions about current events, weather, and more.
data_description.web_search_backend: DuckDuckGo is free but unofficial — it may occasionally be unavailable. Brave Search is recommended.
data_description.brave_api_key: Free tier: 2,000 requests/month. Get a key at brave.com/search/api
data_description.max_results: Maximum number of search results to include in the AI's context.

data_description.searxng_url: URL of your self-hosted SearXNG instance (e.g. https://search.example.com).

data.use_ha_mcp: Connect to Home Assistant's built-in MCP server

data_description.system_prompt: Optional instructions added to every conversation. Leave blank to use the default.
data_description.context_window: Maximum context your model supports. Check your model's documentation.
data_description.summarization_enabled: When conversations get long, AI Plugin summarizes older messages to preserve memory.
data_description.voice_mode: Shorter responses optimized for text-to-speech. Enabled by default for voice assistant interactions.
data_description.xml_fallback: For models that don't support native tool calling. May produce unreliable results.
data_description.response_timeout: How long to wait for the AI before giving up.
data_description.use_ha_mcp: Gives AI Plugin access to your sensors, devices, and automations via HA's built-in MCP server.
data_description.ha_mcp_token: Create one in your HA profile → Long-lived access tokens (scroll to the bottom of the page).

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the provider. Check that your AI provider is running.
error.invalid_api_key: Invalid API key. Check your credentials.
error.searxng_unreachable: Could not reach your SearXNG instance. Check the URL.

mcp_servers.title: MCP Servers

data.mcp_action: What would you like to do?

data_description.mcp_action: Changes are only saved when you choose Save and close.

mcp_add_ha.title: Add Home Assistant MCP Server
mcp_add_ha.description:
Detected HA URL: {ha_mcp_url}

Create a Long-lived access token in your HA profile (Settings → scroll to the bottom of your profile page).

data_description.ha_mcp_token: Required. Paste the token from your HA profile here.

mcp_add_http.title: Add HTTP MCP Server
mcp_add_http.description:
For Home Assistant's built-in MCP endpoint:
  URL: http://YOUR-HA-IP:8123/api/mcp
  Token: create a Long-lived access token in your HA profile (bottom of the page).

data_description.mcp_token: Required for HA's MCP endpoint. Leave blank for unauthenticated servers.

mcp_add_stdio.title: Add stdio MCP Server
mcp_add_stdio.description:
Runs a local MCP tool as a subprocess.
Example: command = uvx   args = mcp-server-time

data_description.mcp_command: The executable to run (e.g. uvx, npx, python).

data_description.mcp_token: Leave blank to remove an existing token.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the provider. Check that your AI provider is running.
error.invalid_api_key: Invalid API key. Check your credentials.
error.searxng_unreachable: Could not reach your SearXNG instance. Check the URL.
